sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root יָקַשׁ)-

(1) a noose, a snare, by which wild beasts and birds are caught, Amo 3:5. מֹוקְשֵׁי־מָוֶת the snares of death, Psa 18:6. Once used of an iron ring put through the nostrils of a beast (comp. חֹוחַ, חָח), Job 40:24.

(2) Metaph. used of a cause of injury. Exo 10:7, עַד־מָתַי יִהְיֶה זֶה לָנוּ לְמֹוקֵשׁ “how long shall this man be a snare to us?” i.e. bring us into evil. Exo 23:33 34:12 Deu 7:16 Jos 23:13 Isa 8:15.

Plur. ־ִים, once ־ֹות Psa 141:9.
